---
title: "DataMining--Project"
author: "Bo"
date: "2026-03-24"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


```{r cars}
# split dataset into training and testing
library(caTools)

# set up baseline regression: logistic regression. 
library(dplyr)

# both packages are for multinomial logistic regression
library(VGAM)

# package for neural network algorithm
library(nnet)

# Splitting data into training and testing
library(caTools)

# package for random forest algorithm 
library(randomForest)

# package for adaboosting algorithm
library(adabag)

# package for cross validation
library(caret)

```


```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
data = read.csv("processed.csv", header = TRUE)

data$ca = as.factor(data$ca)
data$thal = as.factor(data$thal)
data$num = as.factor(data$num)
data$sex = as.factor(data$sex)
data$cp = as.factor(data$cp)
data$restecg = as.factor(data$restecg)
data$fbs = as.factor(data$fbs)
data$exang = as.factor(data$exang)
data$slope = as.factor(data$slope)
```


```{r}
dim(data)
str(data)
```

```{r}
# to check ca and thal levels since there is special character in it. 
unique(data$ca)
unique(data$thal)
```


```{r}
# Find column names where "?" exists
cols_with_question_mark = names(data)[colSums(data == "?", na.rm = TRUE) > 0]

# Print the names of the columns
print(cols_with_question_mark)
```




```{r}
# to check the full dataset, especially the dependent variable
#View(data)
```


```{r}
# check if there is missing values of any features.
#sum(is.na(data))

```


```{r}
#multinomial logistic regression using vgam package
logistic = vglm(as.f)
```
